The modes which are influencing us based on the conception of life are, so to say, triggering different kinds of thoughts, different kinds of impulses, and we helplessly are acting based on those impulses.

So when we are in bodily concept of life, or material concept of life, naturally the thoughts would be of, I am the body, and since our natural desire is to seek happiness, anandamayo bhyasat, I am the body, so the only definition of pleasure is to seek happiness through our senses, or sense gratification.
And since the bottom line, the common definition of pleasure is to seek happiness through our senses, or sense gratification.
The common denominator in all our lives is we all seek happiness. Everyone is engaged in different activities. Ultimately, it's to seek happiness.
So naturally, in the bodily concept of life, sense gratification becomes rampant. That becomes the goal of life. Eat, drink, and be merry. That becomes the philosophy of life.
And invariably, the soul comes under the clutches of karma. More sense gratification.

Krodha, and when sense gratification is obstructed or is not fulfilled, because you cannot get whatever you want, so then krodha comes, frustration comes, anger comes, krodha, dbhavati, sammoha, sammoha, smriti, vibrama, buddhinasho.
So one gets into that.
The connective spiral from the bodily concept, sense gratification, sense gratification, not necessarily what you desire will be met.
So frustration comes, one gets caught in this whirlpool of frustration, anger, unlimited desires.
Another symptom of this bodily concept of life is,
we will hanker for name, fame, adoration, puja, putishtha, labha.
Our thoughts would trigger us to act in ways whereby we will seek name, fame, and adoration.
Actually, if you see this description, everyone in this world is under the clutches of this bodily concept of life.
That's why everyone wants to be famous, wants to gratify the senses.
The whole advancement is how we can titillate our senses more and more in much more gorgeous ways, much more elaborate ways, much more sophisticated ways.
